notes: Smallest round-trip for kiro-cli: one user prompt, one text-only assistant reply, no tool use, asserting the ready→working→ready arc. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE TUI driver (kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools): headless --no-interactive writes NO session file and is invisible to the daemon (FINDINGS.md §7), and the REPL must outlive the observation window so the working→ready settle is captured. kiro writes its <uuid>.jsonl lazily (only after the first prompt), so the on-time ready birth is the pre-session (proc-<PID>) row and the UUID session reconciles in on first input; the working/ready flip then plays out on the UUID session. turn_end anchors to turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the earlier birth ready. The scenario's TotalTokens > 0 criterion is NOT expressible/observable for kiro-cli — the JSONL carries no token/cost/model fields (sidecar holds credits + model_id; the parser does not read it), so the observations vector asserts tokens_nonzero=false, cost_nonzero=false, model unknown.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
UUID session reconciles (different session_id) after the lazy transcript appears, born ready
PID bound to the UUID session
User prompt → working on the UUID session
Text-only assistant reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
